provenance.source_corpus: Animal Colonization of Restored Peatlands: Inoculation of Plant Material as a Source of Insects

nodes.name: active or passive dispersal from the surrounding area

source_spans.text: Insects likely recolonize by active or passive dispersal from the surrounding area.
Insects likely recolonize by active or passive dispersal from the surrounding area.
Diapause may be a major factor for emergence success among seasons of collection.
Diapause may be a major factor for emergence success among seasons of collection.

nodes.name: inoculation with vegetation from another peatland in the same region

source_spans.text: a mined site could be inoculated with vegetation from another peatland in the same region and this would not affect the insect assemblages at the initial stage of establishment.

nodes.name: insect assemblages at the initial stage of establishment

source_spans.text: a mined site could be inoculated with vegetation from another peatland in the same region and this would not affect the insect assemblages at the initial stage of establishment.

nodes.name: vegetation collected and spread on the bog in September

source_spans.text: If vegetation is collected and spread on the bog in September, mortality of diapausing stages is likely to be higher because of exposure on the surface.
If vegetation is collected and spread on the bog in September, mortality of diapausing stages is likely to be higher because of exposure on the surface.
Aspects of the restoration process (depth of collection, shredding and stockpiling of vegetation) could have a direct impact by killing the insects or altering requirements for breaking dormancy.
